data.content:
### Install @dnd-kit/sortable using npm

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/packages/sortable/README.md

This command installs the @dnd-kit/sortable preset package into your project using npm. It adds the necessary dependencies to enable sortable functionalities in your application.

```shell
npm install @dnd-kit/sortable
```

--------------------------------

### Create a snap-to-grid modifier using @dnd-kit's createSnapModifier

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/packages/modifiers/README.md

Illustrates the use of `createSnapModifier` from `@dnd-kit/modifiers` to generate a modifier that snaps draggable items to a defined grid size. This function takes the grid size in pixels as an argument and returns a reusable modifier.

```javascript
import {createSnapModifier} from '@dnd-kit/modifiers';

const gridSize = 20; // pixels
const snapToGridModifier = createSnapModifier(gridSize);
```

--------------------------------

### Apply @dnd-kit modifiers to DndContext and DragOverlay in React

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/packages/modifiers/README.md

Demonstrates how to integrate built-in modifiers like `restrictToVerticalAxis` and `restrictToWindowEdges` with `DndContext` and `DragOverlay` components in a React application. Modifiers are passed as an array to the `modifiers` prop of these components to control drag behavior.

```jsx
import {DndContext, DragOverlay} from '@dnd-kit';
import {
  restrictToVerticalAxis,
  restrictToWindowEdges,
} from '@dnd-kit/modifiers';

function App() {
  return (
    <DndContext modifiers={[restrictToVerticalAxis]}>
      {/* ... */}
      <DragOverlay modifiers={[restrictToWindowEdges]}>{/* ... */}</DragOverlay>
    </DndContext>
  );
}
```

--------------------------------

### Implement a custom snap-to-grid modifier function for dnd-kit

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/packages/modifiers/README.md

Provides an example of building a custom modifier function `snapToGrid` that rounds the `transform` coordinates to the nearest grid size. This custom implementation shows how modifiers receive an `args` object containing the current `transform` and should return a new `transform` object.

```javascript
const gridSize = 20;

function snapToGrid(args) {
  const {transform} = args;

  return {
    ...transform,
    x: Math.ceil(transform.x / gridSize) * gridSize,
    y: Math.ceil(transform.y / gridSize) * gridSize,
  };
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install @dnd-kit/modifiers package

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/packages/modifiers/README.md

Installs the `@dnd-kit/modifiers` package using npm, providing access to various drag-and-drop movement modifiers for `dnd-kit` projects.

```shell
npm install @dnd-kit/modifiers
```

--------------------------------

### Start dnd-kit Parcel Playground (yarn)

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/README.md

Launches the Parcel-powered playground for the dnd-kit project, which becomes accessible on `localhost:1234`. When run in parallel with a lerna watch process, this setup enables hot reloading of changes made to any imported local modules. It's crucial to use the `alias` object in `package.json` for proper resolution of local packages and to prevent duplicate React errors.

```sh
yarn start:playground
```

--------------------------------

### Install dnd-kit Monorepo Dependencies with Yarn

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/README.md

This command installs all necessary dependencies for the dnd-kit monorepo. As the repository uses Lerna and Yarn Workspaces, this command ensures all projects are built and symlinked correctly. npm CLI is not supported for this installation process.

```sh
yarn install
```

--------------------------------

### Start dnd-kit Development Server with Watch Mode (yarn)

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/README.md

Initiates the development workflow for the dnd-kit project. This command builds each package into its respective 'dist' directory and runs the project in watch mode. Any edits saved within a package's 'src' directory will trigger an automatic rebuild to its 'dist' directory, with results streamed to the terminal.

```sh
yarn start
```

--------------------------------

### Run dnd-kit Storybook (yarn)

Source: https://github.com/clauderic/dnd-kit/blob/master/README.md

Starts the Storybook server for the dnd-kit project. Once the server is running, Storybook components can be viewed and interacted with in a web browser by navigating to http://localhost:6006.

```sh
yarn start:storybook
```

=== COMPLETE CONTENT === This response contains all available snippets from this library. No additional content exists. Do not make further requests.
